name: The First State Bank Of Red Wing
description_short: Community-focused bank with three Minnesota locations offering personal and business banking, online services, and local decision-making for loans and community support.
description_long:
First State Bank of Red Wing is a locally owned and operated community bank serving Minnesota with three branch locations in Red Wing, Mazeppa, and Medford. The bank emphasizes its commitment to community-responsive banking, where credit decisions, deposit matters, and community support requests are made locally rather than by distant corporate entities. This localized approach allows the bank to tailor services to specific community needs.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of banking services including personal checking and savings accounts, online personal banking with bill pay and fund transfers, business banking products and services, and secure online banking accessible 24/7. Customers can access accounts through lobby visits during business hours or through drive-up windows with extended hours. The bank is FDIC-insured, providing federal deposit protection. Additional features include check ordering, lost/stolen card reporting, fraud prevention alerts, and educational resources about common banking scams.

What distinguishes First State Bank of Red Wing is its explicit positioning as a "community bank" with decision-making authority retained locally. This contrasts with larger regional or national banks where lending decisions may be made remotely. The bank actively participates in local volunteer projects, service clubs, and youth organizations, positioning itself as a community resource beyond traditional banking functions. The three-location footprint across smaller Minnesota towns (Red Wing, Mazeppa, Medford) reflects a focus on underserved rural markets.

The bank's primary limitation is its geographic reach—limited to three physical locations in Minnesota means customers outside this service area cannot access branch services. The website provides limited transparency about specific interest rates, fees, loan products, or business banking terms, requiring customers to contact branches directly for detailed pricing. Marketing materials emphasize relationship banking rather than competitive rates or technology innovation, suggesting this may not appeal to customers seeking cutting-edge digital banking or best-rate shopping.
services: Online personal banking with login and enrollment
Fraud prevention alerts and scam education
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Local decision-making authority for credit and deposit matters ensures faster turnaround on loan applications
Three branch locations with extended drive-up hours (some locations open until 5 p.m. weekdays and 9 a.m.-noon Saturday)
24/7 online banking access with bill pay and fund transfer capabilities
Active community involvement in volunteer projects, service clubs, and youth organizations
Dedicated fraud prevention and scam education resources for customers
Business banking products designed with operational needs and cost savings in mind
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three Minnesota towns—no service outside Red Wing, Mazeppa, and Medford area
Website lacks transparent information on interest rates, account fees, and specific loan products—requires direct contact with branch
No mention of mobile app or advanced digital banking features compared to larger banks or neobanks
Limited branch hours (lobbies close at 3-5 p.m. most days), which may inconvenience working customers
No information about ATM network access or availability of ATMs outside branch locations

contact.address: 3209 S Service Dr, Red Wing, MN 55066

best_for: Residents of Red Wing, Mazeppa, or Medford, Minnesota seeking relationship-based community banking
Small business owners in rural Minnesota who benefit from local loan decision-making
Customers who value personal service and local community involvement over digital-first banking
Individuals seeking FDIC-insured deposit protection in a locally accountable institution
diagnosis: First State Bank of Red Wing is best for residents of its three-location service area in Minnesota (Red Wing, Mazeppa, Medford) who prioritize community relationships and local decision-making over digital convenience or competitive rate shopping. The main caveat is severe geographic limitation—the bank only serves customers within these specific Minnesota communities and provides minimal online transparency about rates and fees, requiring branch contact for detailed product information.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 3209 S Service Dr, Red Wing, MN 55066
